methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $2.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $2.3B.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Exotec is a Series D robotics and manufacturing company currently valued at $2.0B post-money, having raised $335M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable, all operating in the robotics and manufacturing sector at similar or adjacent stages.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D advancement to the $2.0B base, implying a $3.0B valuation. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Divergent ($2.3B, Series E), Hadrian ($1.6B, Series C), and Nimble Robotics ($1.0B, Series C)—yields a median of $1.6B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $2.3B.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.4B (low) to $3.4B (high), with a median of $2.3B. The wide spread reflects inherent uncertainty in stage-progression methodology relative to peer comparability. The current $2.0B valuation falls within the triangulated range but below the median, suggesting limited upside to the next financing unless the company demonstrates material revenue growth or market-share gains relative to the peer cohort. Key uncertainties include peer company revenue multiples and the applicability of Series E comparables to a Series D company's near-term progression.
